title: Benchmark indices fall amid rising crude oil prices and West Asia conflict
summary: India's benchmark indices ended lower on Wednesday, as crude oil prices increased. The Nifty50 and Sensex experienced significant drops, falling below key levels. Midcap and smallcap stocks also saw declines, reflecting broad market weakness. Most sector indices ended in negative territory, except for FMCG and Auto. Brent crude oil prices rose, impacting investor sentiment across the market.

title: D-St set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty signals weak start

Analysts expect sideways trading with a negative bias due to global tensions. Foreign portfolio investors were net sellers, impacting market sentiment negatively. The Indian rupee fell to a two-month low as oil prices climbed.

title: Pharma stocks slide as Trump's 100% generic drug tariff plan spooks investors
summary: Indian drug shares declined Wednesday following US President Trump's tariff announcement.  These proposed tariffs will significantly impact companies with substantial US revenue exposure.  Analysts suggest investors should now focus on domestically oriented Indian pharmaceutical firms.  The Nifty Pharma index saw a notable decrease, while the benchmark Nifty 50 also fell.  Negotiations are expected given India's vital role in supplying affordable medicines to the US.

title: Mauritius tax protocol to give Indian taxman more powers to probe offshore entities
summary: Mauritius cabinet ratified a tax protocol granting Indian officials more questioning powers.  This allows direct challenges to offshore entities suspected of tax evasion.  The principal purpose test will deny treaty benefits if tax avoidance is a primary aim.  This change follows the Tiger Global verdict and strengthens genuine substance requirements.  The protocol aims to ensure treaty benefits are for legitimate commercial purposes.

title: External sectors steady despite geopolitical uncertainties: RBI
summary: India's external sector shows steady improvement, boosted by foreign investment inflows.  The economy remains strong despite global uncertainties and trade disruptions.  Foreign exchange reserves provide ample cover for imports and external debt.  Exports and imports saw strong growth in the first quarter of this fiscal year.  Bilateral trade agreements will further enhance India's external trade momentum.
